name: An attempt was made to set the Directory Services Restore Mode administrator password
definition: Event ID 4794 records an attempt to set the Directory Services Restore Mode (DSRM) local administrator password on a domain controller. It is a rare, high-signal Windows Security event.
trigger_scenarios: It is generated when an account attempts to set the DSRM administrator password on a domain controller. Outside scheduled disaster-recovery or domain-controller maintenance, it should be exceptional.

key_fields.explanation: The account initiating the operation. It must map to an authorized domain controller administrator and a known change ticket.
The computer name from which the password-setting attempt originated. Compare it with approved domain-controller administration hosts.
The hexadecimal result of the attempt. Status 0x0 means the operation succeeded; other values require Windows error-code interpretation.

false_positives: Ticketed disaster-recovery drills that rotate the DSRM password are the usual legitimate source.
New domain controller promotion can set an initial DSRM password during a documented deployment window.
detection_notes: T1098 Account Manipulation applies when an unauthorized operator attempts to set the Directory Services Restore Mode administrator password. Treat one unexpected 4794 on a domain controller as high priority: Status 0x0 means the operation succeeded, so confirm SubjectUserName, SubjectLogonId, and Workstation against an approved recovery-maintenance change; nonzero status values still warrant review as attempted access.
kql_snippet:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4794
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, SubjectLogonId, EventData
| order by TimeGenerated desc
spl_snippet:
index=wineventlog EventCode=4794
| table _time, host, SubjectUserName, SubjectLogonId, Workstation, Status
sample_log:
Subject: Account Name: CORP\admin.ops
Logon ID: 0x36f67
Workstation: DC01
Status: 0x0

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Event ID 4794
queries.query:
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4794
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, EventData
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Windows Security Events connector populates SecurityEvent.
Apply the event-specific field and correlation guidance on this page after retrieving the base events.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Event ID 4794
queries.query:
index=wineventlog source="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4794
| table _time, host, user, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and field aliases vary by Splunk deployment and Windows add-on version.
Map provider fields from the raw event before adding event-specific filtering or aggregation.

queries.title: Retrieve Windows Event ID 4794
assumptions: The Windows Security channel is ingested into Cortex XDR.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Windows Security Event ID 4794
queries.query:
title: Windows Security Event ID 4794
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: security
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 4794
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the field conditions and correlations documented on this page in the destination SIEM.

value_references.meaning: The attempt to set the DSRM administrator password succeeded.
value_references.security_relevance: A successful operation outside an approved recovery-maintenance workflow changes a sensitive domain-controller recovery credential and requires immediate validation.

faqs.question: What does Windows Event ID 4794 mean?
faqs.answer: Event ID 4794 records an attempt to set the Directory Services Restore Mode administrator password on a domain controller and includes the actor, originating workstation, and status.

faqs.question: Does 4794 show whether the password-reset attempt succeeded?
faqs.answer: Yes. Status 0x0 means the operation succeeded; a nonzero status represents a failed attempt that should be interpreted using the corresponding Windows error code.

faqs.question: Why is DSRM password activity sensitive?
faqs.answer: The DSRM administrator is a local recovery account on a domain controller, so changing its password affects a credential that is separate from ordinary domain-account passwords and should be tightly controlled.

sources.title: 4794: Microsoft Windows security auditing event reference
